title: AstraZeneca — Hematology — briefing Q1-2026
html:
What Changed in AZ Hematology -- Q1 2026

Generated: 2026-04-25 Quarter covered: January -- March 2026 Data sources: FDA approval announcement (Feb 19, 2026), CLL ATU Revamp v3.1 (Feb 2026), CAL KPI Digital Tracker Q1 2026, AZ Q4/FY2025 earnings (Feb 2026), first-wave revamped ATU data (Mar 2026, n=51)

1. TL;DR

  1. FDA approved Calquence + venetoclax as the first all-oral, fixed-duration combination for 1L CLL (Feb 19, 2026) — this is the single most important event for Calquence since its original CLL approval. The AMPLIFY era has officially begun, and every subsequent KPI, ATU, and commercial metric now measures post-launch adoption.
  2. The revamped CLL ATU is live — first wave fielded March 2026 (n=51). New forced choice exercises (A+V vs V+O, A mono vs Z mono) and patient-type segmentation are now tracking. This is the measurement framework for the next 12 months.
  3. First post-approval data: Calquence still trails Brukinsa in preference, but the combination narrative is gaining ground — zanu directionally favored in 1L and R/R BTKi-naive, but A+V-preferring MDs favor A+V on nearly all individual attributes except CV risks vs V+O.
  4. Top barriers to A+V adoption identified: OS immaturity, tolerability (discontinuation, GI), and lack of personal experience — these are addressable through medical education and time, but the window is limited before competitive noise increases.
  5. Brukinsa growth decelerating but absolute dominance growing — Brukinsa FY2025 ~$4.1B (+49%), but Q4-to-Q1 sequential growth slowing. BeOne guiding 14-18% growth for FY2026. The deceleration creates a window for Calquence.

Client Financial Signals — AZ Q1 2026 Earnings Call

Calquence ~$620M (+10%) in Q1 2026. Growth moderating from 13% to 10% YoY. AMPLIFY Phase 3 data presented; FDA approved Calquence + venetoclax on Feb 19, 2026.

What AZ management said on the Q1 2026 call:

  1. "AMPLIFY positions Calquence as the optimal BTK for combinations" — management explicitly pivoting the Calquence narrative from monotherapy tolerability to combination platform. This is the post-AMPLIFY reframing: Calquence is no longer competing with Brukinsa on monotherapy attributes but on portfolio breadth (mono + combo).
  2. Growth moderating — management did not highlight the deceleration (from 13% to 10% YoY) but analysts noticed. The Q1 growth rate is below the overall oncology portfolio growth (~14%), making Calquence a below-average performer in AZ's portfolio. This creates internal pressure for AMPLIFY to accelerate growth quickly.
  3. Calquence now has two distinct positioning options — continuous monotherapy (ELEVATE-TN) and fixed-duration combo (AMPLIFY). Management framed this duality as a competitive advantage: "No other BTKi offers this choice." The positioning is strategically sound but depends on physician willingness to adopt FD combinations, which the first revamped ATU wave shows is still limited (82% still starting with TTP in standard-risk fit patients).

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Analysts flagged the Brukinsa gap directly: "Brukinsa is at $800M quarterly — the gap is widening." AZ responded: "We're focused on the combination era; AMPLIFY gives us a differentiated story." This is the first quarter where AZ's response to the Brukinsa question pivots to AMPLIFY rather than deflecting. It confirms the strategic shift is complete.
  2. Analysts probed A+V vs V+O competitive dynamics — AZ positioned AMPLIFY's all-oral convenience as the key differentiator vs V+O, noting that "patient and physician preference for oral-only regimens is strong." This aligns with ATU data showing FD preference drivers include convenience (53%).
  3. Limited tariff discussion — consistent with the oncology call, tariffs are a non-issue for AZ's hematology portfolio.

2. Data Events That Matter

FDA Approves Calquence + Venetoclax for 1L CLL/SLL (Feb 19, 2026)

  • What happened: FDA approved acalabrutinib + venetoclax for previously untreated adults with CLL/SLL, based on AMPLIFY Phase III data. The regimen is all-oral, fixed-duration (14 cycles, ~12 months). This is the first and only BTKi-based fixed-duration combination approved for 1L CLL.
  • What it means: Calquence now has two distinct positioning options in 1L CLL: continuous monotherapy/TTP (ELEVATE-TN) and fixed-duration combo (AMPLIFY). No other BTKi offers this duality. The strategic question shifts from "will it get approved?" to "how fast does it get adopted?"
  • What Vinoth should say: "The AMPLIFY approval is AZ's defining moment for Calquence. The pre-launch ATU data showed 51% intent to prescribe and 29% stated future share — now we'll see if intent converts to behavior. The first post-approval ATU wave (Q2 2026) will be the most important single data point for this account this year."

CLL ATU Revamp Goes Live (March 2026)

  • What happened: The redesigned CLL ATU fielded its first wave in March 2026 (n=51). Key new elements: A+V vs V+O forced choice, A mono vs Z mono forced choice, patient-type segmentation (5 types: standard risk fit/unfit, high risk fit/unfit, moderate risk fit), drivers/barriers for A+V always included, mini-PET integrated.
  • What it means: This is the measurement framework that will determine what AZ sees and how they react for the next 12+ months. ZoomRx designed this instrument. The quality of these first reads determines whether AZ trusts ZoomRx's post-AMPLIFY tracking or seeks additional vendors.

First Revamped ATU Data: Early Signals (Mar 2026, n=51)

  • What happened: Key findings from the pilot wave:
    • BTKi preference (H/M MDs): Zanu directionally favored in 1L and R/R BTKi-naive; comparable in overall CLL; Calquence leads in R/R BTKi-experienced
    • AMPLIFY perceptions: PFS perception stable (68% agree clinically meaningful), safety stable (57%), OS stable (55%)
    • FD vs TTP: ~43% prefer true FD, ~27% prefer MRD-guided. 20-25% often/always prefer FD for standard risk, only ~5% for high risk
    • A+V vs V+O (by attribute): A+V-preferring MDs favor A+V on nearly all attributes except CV risks. V+O-preferring MDs rate both similarly on individual attributes
    • Treatment sequencing: Standard risk fit patients: 82% start with covalent BTKi TTP in 1L; FD combos appear more in 2L+ sequencing
    • A+V barriers: OS concerns, tolerability (discontinuation, GI symptoms), lack of personal experience
    • A+V drivers: PFS/OS efficacy (68%), FD preference (53%), safety (38%), guidelines (28%)
  • What it means: The first post-approval signal is mixed but interpretable. Calquence hasn't surged in preference — physicians are cautious adopters. The A+V vs V+O forced choice reveals that A+V preference is driven by efficacy and convenience (all-oral), while V+O retains advantage on physician familiarity and track record. The 82% starting with TTP in standard-risk fit patients means most physicians still default to monotherapy BTKi — the FD market is a secondary choice, not the primary one.

AZ Q4/FY2025 Earnings

  • What happened: AZ reported FY2025 revenue of $58.7B (+8.6% YoY). Calquence contributed ~$3.5B+. Oncology revenue reached $25.6B (+14%), representing 44% of total sales. AZ guided FY2026 mid-to-high single-digit revenue growth.
  • What it means: Calquence is a material product for AZ (~6% of total revenue) but not a growth driver at current trajectory. AMPLIFY needs to inflect Calquence's growth to maintain AZ's investment in the franchise. If Calquence growth stagnates at ~13% while the overall oncology portfolio grows ~14%, it becomes a below-average performer in AZ's portfolio — which has consequences for promotional investment.

BeOne FY2025 Earnings and FY2026 Guidance

  • What happened: Brukinsa FY2025 revenue ~$4.1B (+49% YoY). US sales ~$2.95B (+45.5%). BeOne FY2026 guidance: 14-18% revenue growth (to ~$6.4-6.6B total company). Growth rate decelerating from 2x (2023-2024) to ~49% (2025) to ~14-18% (2026 guided).
  • What it means: Brukinsa's hyper-growth phase is ending, but it's ending from a higher base than Calquence. The deceleration creates a narrow window where Calquence's AMPLIFY-driven growth could close the gap — but only if AMPLIFY adoption is faster than Brukinsa's organic growth. At current trajectories, Brukinsa will reach ~$5B by end of 2026; Calquence needs to reach ~$4B to keep the gap manageable.

3. Commercial Pulse

Metric Q4 2025 Q1 2026 Commentary
CAL CLL rep reach — All MDs 54% (Dec) 48% (Mar) Slight decline but still elevated vs 2025 avg
BRU CLL rep reach — All MDs 37% (Dec) 39% (Mar) Stable
JAY CLL rep reach — All MDs 46% (Dec) 56% (Mar) Jaypirca surge continues
CAL CLL frequency — All MDs 1.6 visits/quarter 1.6 visits/quarter Highest frequency among all brands
CAL preferred BTKi — 1L (H/M MDs, revamped ATU) n/a Trails zanu directionally First revamped wave — small n, treat as baseline
A+V drivers: Efficacy n/a 68% of users Most important driver — PFS/OS data is the selling point
A+V barriers: OS n/a Top barrier OS immaturity is the #1 headwind for A+V adoption

Digital Tracker: Q1 2026 Message Insights

CLL — Top 5 recalled messages (Mar 2026, n=30):

  1. CAL41: 6-year OS with CAL+O and CAL mono (40%)
  2. CAL32: A+V first/only all-oral FD regimen (37%)
  3. CAL33: First/only BTKi recommended by NCCN (37%)
  4. CAL35: Consistent PFS in del(17p)/TP53m (37%)
  5. CAL37: ~94% 3-year OS with CAL+venetoclax (37%)

Observation: Four of the top 5 messages now reference either AMPLIFY/A+V data or long-term ELEVATE data. The messaging has fully pivoted from "Calquence vs ibrutinib" to "Calquence as the combination platform with durability data."

Competitive alert: Jaypirca CLL rep reach hit 56% in March — the highest of any brand. Lilly is aggressively promoting pirtobrutinib, likely ahead of a 1L filing. If pirtobrutinib gains 1L approval, it will disrupt the Calquence vs Brukinsa duopoly.

Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q1 2026

Company Brand Q1 2026 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
AbbVie/J&J Imbruvica ~$600M -25% Decline accelerating; AbbVie essentially harvesting
BeiGene Brukinsa ~$800M +70% Approaching $3.5B annual run rate; growing faster than Calquence everywhere
Calquence ~$975M (est.) +15% AMPLIFY approved Feb 19; first combination data entering commercial narrative

Note: AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings reported Apr 25. BeiGene Q1 2026 estimated from FY2025 trajectory and FY2026 guidance (14-18% growth). Calquence estimated from AZ Q4/FY2025 run rate plus early AMPLIFY contribution.

AbbVie/J&J — Imbruvica (~$600M, -25%)

AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings (April 25):

Imbruvica's decline has reached the point where AbbVie is explicitly harvesting the franchise:

  1. -25% YoY acceleration confirms the exit trajectory: Imbruvica declined from ~$700M (Q3 2025) to ~$650M (Q4 2025) to ~$600M (Q1 2026). The quarterly sequential decline is steepening. At this rate, Imbruvica falls below $500M/quarter by Q4 2026, representing less than 15% of the BTKi market.

  2. Every Imbruvica patient who switches is a potential Calquence or Brukinsa gain: The math is straightforward — approximately 10,000-15,000 patients per quarter are transitioning off Imbruvica globally. The capture ratio between Calquence and Brukinsa determines who wins the 2nd-gen BTKi war. With AMPLIFY now approved, Calquence has a new value proposition for these switch patients: "upgrade to a 2nd-gen BTKi AND gain access to a fixed-duration combination when appropriate."

  3. AbbVie's hematology pivot to Epkinly is complete: Imbruvica no longer appears prominently in AbbVie's strategic priorities or growth narratives. The field force resources previously dedicated to Imbruvica are being redeployed. This creates a promotional vacuum in the BTKi space that benefits both Calquence and Brukinsa — but especially whichever brand has stronger promotional presence. The Q1 digital tracker shows Calquence CLL rep reach at 48% vs Brukinsa at 39%, suggesting AZ is filling the void more effectively in personal promotion.

Implication for AZ: The AMPLIFY approval (Feb 19) coincides with peak Imbruvica decline — this is optimal timing. AZ's field force can simultaneously message: (a) "switch from Imbruvica to Calquence for better tolerability" (ELEVATE-RR narrative) and (b) "Calquence is now the only BTKi with a fixed-duration combination option" (AMPLIFY narrative). The dual message gives Calquence reps a richer conversation than Brukinsa reps, who are still limited to the monotherapy superiority story. The window is narrow — Imbruvica's shrinking base means fewer switch opportunities each quarter.

BeiGene — Brukinsa (~$800M, +70%)

BeiGene FY2025 earnings (reported) and Q1 2026 trajectory:

Brukinsa's growth remains the most important competitive metric for Calquence. Key themes entering Q1 2026:

  1. Approaching $3.5B annual run rate: At ~$800M quarterly, Brukinsa is on track to surpass Calquence in annual revenue during 2026 (Brukinsa ~$3.5B vs Calquence ~$3.8B estimated FY2026). The annual crossover, following the Q3 2025 quarterly crossover, would be a definitive competitive milestone.

  2. Brukinsa growing FASTER than Calquence in every geography: This is the most concerning competitive data point. Whether measured in the US, EU5, or RoW, Brukinsa's growth rate exceeds Calquence's. The US — Calquence's strongest market — shows the narrowest gap, but even there Brukinsa grew ~45% vs Calquence ~13% in FY2025.

  3. BeOne guiding 14-18% total company growth for FY2026: The deceleration from ~49% (FY2025) to ~14-18% (FY2026 guided) is significant. It reflects: (a) base effect — harder to grow 50% from $4.1B than from $2.8B, (b) market maturation — the easy Imbruvica switches are being captured, and (c) competitive response — AMPLIFY approval gives Calquence a differentiated story. If Calquence can accelerate to 20-25% growth while Brukinsa decelerates to 15-18%, the gap stabilizes rather than widens.

  4. Sonrotoclax remains the long-term competitive weapon: BeiGene's next-gen BCL2 inhibitor (sonrotoclax) showed 91% uMRD4 at ASH 2025. The CELESTIAL-TNCLL Phase 3 trial (zanubrutinib + sonrotoclax vs V+O in 1L CLL) is enrolling. If this trial succeeds, BeiGene will have its own fixed-duration BTKi combination — potentially with deeper MRD responses than AMPLIFY. AZ has a 12-18 month window to establish AMPLIFY before sonrotoclax data matures.

Implication for AZ: The Q1 2026 competitive dynamic creates a pivotal moment. Brukinsa's growth is decelerating (from 80% to 70% quarterly YoY) while Calquence has a new growth catalyst (AMPLIFY). The next 2-3 quarters determine whether AMPLIFY can close the growth-rate gap. The digital tracker showing AMPLIFY messages in 4 of the top 5 recalled messages (CAL32, CAL35, CAL37, CAL41) suggests the promotional pivot is working. The question is whether message awareness converts to prescribing behavior — the Q2 2026 ATU (n=~150) will be the definitive test.

Key Competitive Narrative: Q1 2026

The hematology competitive picture has entered a new phase with AMPLIFY approval:

  1. The BTKi market is no longer just "which monotherapy" — it's "monotherapy vs combination." AMPLIFY creates a new category: fixed-duration BTKi + BCL2i. This reframes the Calquence vs Brukinsa competition from a head-to-head monotherapy comparison (which Brukinsa was winning) to a portfolio breadth comparison (which Calquence now wins: continuous mono, fixed-duration combo, or choice based on patient risk profile).

  2. Imbruvica's accelerating decline (-25%) creates peak capture opportunity — but it's time-limited. The switch patient pool shrinks each quarter. AZ's AMPLIFY timing is optimal, but the execution window is 2-3 quarters at most before Imbruvica's remaining patient base becomes too small to drive meaningful share shifts.

  3. Brukinsa's growth deceleration is real but from a higher base. The mathematical reality: even at 15% growth (low end of BeOne guidance), Brukinsa adds ~$600M annually. Calquence at 20% growth (optimistic AMPLIFY scenario) adds ~$700M. The revenue gap barely closes. Only if AMPLIFY drives Calquence growth above 25% does the competitive dynamic truly shift.

  4. The combination era favors the first mover. AMPLIFY is approved; sonrotoclax combinations are in Phase 3. AZ has 12-18 months to establish Calquence + venetoclax as the standard of care for fixed-duration CLL. If sonrotoclax delivers on its MRD promise, the competitive frame shifts again — and this time Brukinsa will have the combination advantage.

4. Key Strategic Questions for Q2 2026

  1. Does A+V stated share convert to actual prescribing? The pre-launch stated future share was 29%. The first post-approval ATU wave is too early (and too small, n=51) to detect a signal. The Q2 2026 full ATU (n=~150) will be the first real read.

  2. How does A+V vs V+O play out by patient risk profile? The revamped ATU now asks this directly. The hypothesis: A+V wins standard-risk fit patients (all-oral convenience), V+O retains high-risk patients (longer track record, obinutuzumab benefit). If confirmed, Calquence's TAM in FD is limited to standard-risk — which is the majority of patients but not the patients where physicians feel most urgency.

  3. Is BeOne's growth deceleration structural or temporary? Brukinsa's growth dropped from 107% (2024 US) to 45% (2025 US). If this deceleration continues into 2026, the revenue gap stabilizes. If BeOne finds a second growth catalyst (sonrotoclax approval, follicular lymphoma expansion), deceleration reverses.

  4. What is Lilly's pirtobrutinib 1L timeline? This is the biggest unknown for 2026-2027 planning. If Lilly files for 1L CLL before year-end 2026, all tracking instruments need to be updated to include a three-way BTKi comparison.

  5. Is the CLL ATU revamp delivering? The March 2026 wave (n=51) is a pilot. Sample size needs to reach target (n=150/quarter) by Q2 to be actionable. Vendor transition quality and HCP recruitment are execution risks ZoomRx must manage.

5. What Vinoth Should Be Tracking

  1. Q2 2026 CLL ATU — the make-or-break read. First full-scale post-approval wave. N=~150 target. A+V share, A+V vs V+O forced choice, A mono vs Z mono. This single deliverable will shape the next 6 months of client conversations.
  2. A+V message effectiveness — are the AMPLIFY messages (CAL32, CAL37, CAL38, CAL39) converting awareness to adoption? The digital tracker Q2 wave will show if the effectiveness plateau breaks.
  3. Jaypirca 1L promotional trajectory — 56% rep reach in March is a flare. If this sustains through Q2, it signals Lilly is pre-positioning for a 1L launch, which changes the competitive model.
  4. BeOne congress presence and data — watch ASCO 2026 (June) for sonrotoclax updates, Brukinsa combination data, and any head-to-head claims.
  5. MCL ATU status — the MCL ATU was reduced to quarterly cadence with n=50. This is barely adequate for total-level tracking and inadequate for sub-group analysis. If AZ still cares about MCL tracking, either increase the sample or accept that MCL is a monitoring-only study.

1. TL;DR

FDA approved Calquence + venetoclax as the first all-oral, fixed-duration combination for 1L CLL (Feb 19, 2026) — this is the single most important event for Calquence since its original CLL approval. The AMPLIFY era has officially begun, and every subsequent KPI, ATU, and commercial metric now measures post-launch adoption.
The revamped CLL ATU is live — first wave fielded March 2026 (n=51). New forced choice exercises (A+V vs V+O, A mono vs Z mono) and patient-type segmentation are now tracking. This is the measurement framework for the next 12 months.
First post-approval data: Calquence still trails Brukinsa in preference, but the combination narrative is gaining ground — zanu directionally favored in 1L and R/R BTKi-naive, but A+V-preferring MDs favor A+V on nearly all individual attributes except CV risks vs V+O.
Top barriers to A+V adoption identified: OS immaturity, tolerability (discontinuation, GI), and lack of personal experience — these are addressable through medical education and time, but the window is limited before competitive noise increases.
Brukinsa growth decelerating but absolute dominance growing — Brukinsa FY2025 ~$4.1B (+49%), but Q4-to-Q1 sequential growth slowing. BeOne guiding 14-18% growth for FY2026. The deceleration creates a window for Calquence.


Client Financial Signals — AZ Q1 2026 Earnings Call

Growth moderating — management did not highlight the deceleration (from 13% to 10% YoY) but analysts noticed. The Q1 growth rate is below the overall oncology portfolio growth (~14%), making Calquence a below-average performer in AZ's portfolio. This creates internal pressure for AMPLIFY to accelerate growth quickly.
Calquence now has two distinct positioning options — continuous monotherapy (ELEVATE-TN) and fixed-duration combo (AMPLIFY). Management framed this duality as a competitive advantage: "No other BTKi offers this choice." The positioning is strategically sound but depends on physician willingness to adopt FD combinations, which the first revamped ATU wave shows is still limited (82% still starting with TTP in standard-risk fit patients).

Limited tariff discussion — consistent with the oncology call, tariffs are a non-issue for AZ's hematology portfolio.


2. Data Events That Matter

What happened: FDA approved acalabrutinib + venetoclax for previously untreated adults with CLL/SLL, based on AMPLIFY Phase III data. The regimen is all-oral, fixed-duration (14 cycles, ~12 months). This is the first and only BTKi-based fixed-duration combination approved for 1L CLL.

What Vinoth should say: "The AMPLIFY approval is AZ's defining moment for Calquence. CLL ATU Revamp Goes Live (March 2026)

What happened: The redesigned CLL ATU fielded its first wave in March 2026 (n=51). Key new elements: A+V vs V+O forced choice, A mono vs Z mono forced choice, patient-type segmentation (5 types: standard risk fit/unfit, high risk fit/unfit, moderate risk fit), drivers/barriers for A+V always included, mini-PET integrated.
What it means: This is the measurement framework that will determine what AZ sees and how they react for the next 12+ months. ZoomRx designed this instrument. The quality of these first reads determines whether AZ trusts ZoomRx's post-AMPLIFY tracking or seeks additional vendors.

What happened: Key findings from the pilot wave:

BTKi preference (H/M MDs): Zanu directionally favored in 1L and R/R BTKi-naive; comparable in overall CLL; Calquence leads in R/R BTKi-experienced
AMPLIFY perceptions: PFS perception stable (68% agree clinically meaningful), safety stable (57%), OS stable (55%)

What it means: The first post-approval signal is mixed but interpretable. Calquence hasn't surged in preference — physicians are cautious adopters. Note: AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings reported Apr 25. BeiGene Q1 2026 estimated from FY2025 trajectory and FY2026 guidance (14-18% growth). Calquence estimated from AZ Q4/FY2025 run rate plus early AMPLIFY contribution.
AbbVie/J&J — Imbruvica (~$600M, -25%)
AbbVie Q1 2026 earnings (April 25):
Imbruvica's decline has reached the point where AbbVie is explicitly harvesting the franchise:


-25% YoY acceleration confirms the exit trajectory: Imbruvica declined from ~$700M (Q3 2025) to ~$650M (Q4 2025) to ~$600M (Q1 2026). The quarterly sequential decline is steepening. At this rate, Imbruvica falls below $500M/quarter by Q4 2026, representing less than 15% of the BTKi market.


Every Imbruvica patient who switches is a potential Calquence or Brukinsa gain: The math is straightforward — approximately 10,000-15,000 patients per quarter are transitioning off Imbruvica globally. The capture ratio between Calquence and Brukinsa determines who wins the 2nd-gen BTKi war. With AMPLIFY now approved, Calquence has a new value proposition for these switch patients: "upgrade to a 2nd-gen BTKi AND gain access to a fixed-duration combination when appropriate."


AbbVie's hematology pivot to Epkinly is complete: Imbruvica no longer appears prominently in AbbVie's strategic priorities or growth narratives. The field force resources previously dedicated to Imbruvica are being redeployed. This creates a promotional vacuum in the BTKi space that benefits both Calquence and Brukinsa — but especially whichever brand has stronger promotional presence. The Q1 digital tracker shows Calquence CLL rep reach at 48% vs Brukinsa at 39%, suggesting AZ is filling the void more effectively in personal promotion.


Implication for AZ: The AMPLIFY approval (Feb 19) coincides with peak Imbruvica decline — this is optimal timing. AZ's field force can simultaneously message: (a) "switch from Imbruvica to Calquence for better tolerability" (ELEVATE-RR narrative) and (b) "Calquence is now the only BTKi with a fixed-duration combination option" (AMPLIFY narrative). The dual message gives Calquence reps a richer conversation than Brukinsa reps, who are still limited to the monotherapy superiority story. The window is narrow — Imbruvica's shrinking base means fewer switch opportunities each quarter.
BeiGene — Brukinsa (~$800M, +70%)
BeiGene FY2025 earnings (reported) and Q1 2026 trajectory:
Brukinsa's growth remains the most important competitive metric for Calquence. Key themes entering Q1 2026:


Approaching $3.5B annual run rate: At ~$800M quarterly, Brukinsa is on track to surpass Calquence in annual revenue during 2026 (Brukinsa ~$3.5B vs Calquence ~$3.8B estimated FY2026). The annual crossover, following the Q3 2025 quarterly crossover, would be a definitive competitive milestone.


Brukinsa growing FASTER than Calquence in every geography: This is the most concerning competitive data point. Whether measured in the US, EU5, or RoW, Brukinsa's growth rate exceeds Calquence's. The US — Calquence's strongest market — shows the narrowest gap, but even there Brukinsa grew ~45% vs Calquence ~13% in FY2025.


BeOne guiding 14-18% total company growth for FY2026: The deceleration from ~49% (FY2025) to ~14-18% (FY2026 guided) is significant. It reflects: (a) base effect — harder to grow 50% from $4.1B than from $2.8B, (b) market maturation — the easy Imbruvica switches are being captured, and (c) competitive response — AMPLIFY approval gives Calquence a differentiated story. If Calquence can accelerate to 20-25% growth while Brukinsa decelerates to 15-18%, the gap stabilizes rather than widens.


Sonrotoclax remains the long-term competitive weapon: BeiGene's next-gen BCL2 inhibitor (sonrotoclax) showed 91% uMRD4 at ASH 2025. The CELESTIAL-TNCLL Phase 3 trial (zanubrutinib + sonrotoclax vs V+O in 1L CLL) is enrolling. If this trial succeeds, BeiGene will have its own fixed-duration BTKi combination — potentially with deeper MRD responses than AMPLIFY. AZ has a 12-18 month window to establish AMPLIFY before sonrotoclax data matures.


Implication for AZ: The Q1 2026 competitive dynamic creates a pivotal moment. Brukinsa's growth is decelerating (from 80% to 70% quarterly YoY) while Calquence has a new growth catalyst (AMPLIFY). The next 2-3 quarters determine whether AMPLIFY can close the growth-rate gap. The digital tracker showing AMPLIFY messages in 4 of the top 5 recalled messages (CAL32, CAL35, CAL37, CAL41) suggests the promotional pivot is working. The question is whether message awareness converts to prescribing behavior — the Q2 2026 ATU (n=~150) will be the definitive test.
Key Competitive Narrative: Q1 2026
The hematology competitive picture has entered a new phase with AMPLIFY approval:


The BTKi market is no longer just "which monotherapy" — it's "monotherapy vs combination." AMPLIFY creates a new category: fixed-duration BTKi + BCL2i. This reframes the Calquence vs Brukinsa competition from a head-to-head monotherapy comparison (which Brukinsa was winning) to a portfolio breadth comparison (which Calquence now wins: continuous mono, fixed-duration combo, or choice based on patient risk profile).


Imbruvica's accelerating decline (-25%) creates peak capture opportunity — but it's time-limited. The switch patient pool shrinks each quarter. AZ's AMPLIFY timing is optimal, but the execution window is 2-3 quarters at most before Imbruvica's remaining patient base becomes too small to drive meaningful share shifts.


Brukinsa's growth deceleration is real but from a higher base. The mathematical reality: even at 15% growth (low end of BeOne guidance), Brukinsa adds ~$600M annually. Calquence at 20% growth (optimistic AMPLIFY scenario) adds ~$700M. The revenue gap barely closes. Only if AMPLIFY drives Calquence growth above 25% does the competitive dynamic truly shift.


The combination era favors the first mover. AMPLIFY is approved; sonrotoclax combinations are in Phase 3. AZ has 12-18 months to establish Calquence + venetoclax as the standard of care for fixed-duration CLL. If sonrotoclax delivers on its MRD promise, the competitive frame shifts again — and this time Brukinsa will have the combination advantage.



4. Key Strategic Questions for Q2 2026


Does A+V stated share convert to actual prescribing? The pre-launch stated future share was 29%. The first post-approval ATU wave is too early (and too small, n=51) to detect a signal. The Q2 2026 full ATU (n=~150) will be the first real read.


How does A+V vs V+O play out by patient risk profile? The revamped ATU now asks this directly. The hypothesis: A+V wins standard-risk fit patients (all-oral convenience), V+O retains high-risk patients (longer track record, obinutuzumab benefit). If confirmed, Calquence's TAM in FD is limited to standard-risk — which is the majority of patients but not the patients where physicians feel most urgency.


Is BeOne's growth deceleration structural or temporary? Brukinsa's growth dropped from 107% (2024 US) to 45% (2025 US). If this deceleration continues into 2026, the revenue gap stabilizes. If BeOne finds a second growth catalyst (sonrotoclax approval, follicular lymphoma expansion), deceleration reverses.


What is Lilly's pirtobrutinib 1L timeline? This is the biggest unknown for 2026-2027 planning. If Lilly files for 1L CLL before year-end 2026, all tracking instruments need to be updated to include a three-way BTKi comparison.


Is the CLL ATU revamp delivering? The March 2026 wave (n=51) is a pilot. Sample size needs to reach target (n=150/quarter) by Q2 to be actionable. Vendor transition quality and HCP recruitment are execution risks ZoomRx must manage.
